labels.value: Domain Used on Period Dimension
Invalid Relationship Source Object

This error occurs when a relationship has a source object that is not valid for the relationship type. The relationship type defines the allowed source object types, and the error indicates that the actual source object does not conform to these requirements.
Invalid Relationship Target Object

This error occurs when a relationship has a target object that is not valid for the relationship type. The relationship type defines the allowed target object types, and the error indicates that the actual target object does not conform to these requirements.
The relationship '{sourceObject}' is not valid for relationship type '{relationshipType}'.
The relationship '{targetObject}' is not valid for relationship type '{relationshipType}'.
The cube dimension property domainName or domainDataType is not valid when used on the period dimension in cube '{cubeName}'.
Invalid Allow Domain Facts Property On Concept Dimension
The property 'allowDomainFacts' is not valid for concept dimension '{dimensionName}' in cube '{cubeName}'. Concept dimensions cannot have domain facts.
Duplicate Objects
Conflicting Property Values
The object '{objectName}' is defined multiple times in the model.
Property '{xbrl:propertyName}' is defined multiple times on {objectType}, {object} with conflicting values: {conflictingValues}.
Undefined QName Reference
The referenced QName '{qname}' cannot be found in the XBRL model.
Invalid QName Object Type
The referenced QName '{qname}' resolves to an object of an incorrect type. Expected a '{expectedType}' object but found a '{actualType}' object.
Invalid Property Value
The property '{propertyName}' has an invalid value '{value}' for object '{objectName}'.
No Fact Space For Fact
The fact with concept '{conceptName}' does not fit within any defined cube fact space.
Fact Missing From Cube
The cube '{cubeName}' has cubeComplete set to true but at least one fact space defined by the cube has no facts.
Invalid Taxonomy Defined Dimension
The dimension '{dimensionName}' is not properly defined in the XBRL model.
Invalid Cube Type (Removed — use oimte:invalidQNameType)
This error code has been replaced by oimte:invalidQNameType.
Invalid Cube Dimension Domain Name
The domain name '{domainName}' specified for dimension '{dimensionName}' in cube '{cubeName}' is not valid.
Exclude Cube Self Reference
The exclude cube '{excludeCubeQName}' is the same as the cube '{cubeName}'.
Exclude Cube Cannot Be Required Cube
The cube '{cubeName}' includes '{conflictingCubeQName}' in both the excludeCubes and requiredCubes properties.
Exclude Cube Shares Dimensional Space With Required Cube
The cube '{cubeName}' has dimensional spaces that appear in both the excludeCubes (via '{excludeCubeQName}') and requiredCubes (via '{requiredCubeQName}') properties.
Required Cube Dimensional Space Missing From Cube
The cube '{cubeName}' specifies '{requiredCubeQName}' in requiredCubes but at least one fact space defined by the required cube has no facts.
Cube Missing Dimension
The cube '{cubeName}' is missing required dimension '{dimensionName}'.
Cube Missing Concept Dimension
The cube '{cubeName}' is missing the required concept dimension.
Cube Dimension Not Allowed
The dimension '{dimensionName}' is not allowed in cube '{cubeName}' of type '{cubeType}'.
Cube Dimension Duplicated
The dimension '{dimensionName}' is duplicated in cube '{cubeName}'.
Invalid Object Type
The object '{objectName}' has an invalid type '{actualType}' but expected type '{expectedType}'.
Invalid Period Constraint
The constraint on dimension '{dimensionName}' is invalid: {constraintDetails}.
Redundant timeSpan Property
The timeSpan property must not be used with both the endDate and startDate properties in period constraint.
Missing Domain Class Source

Domain '${domain.name}' declares root '${domain.root}' but this root object is never used as a source in any relationship. Every domain class must appear as the source of at least one relationship to define the domain's member hierarchy.
Invalid Network Root
The declared root '${network.root}' of network '${network.name}' does not match the actual root nodes.
Duplicate Relationships

A relationship in network '${network.name}' with source '${relationship.source}', target '${relationship.target}', and order '${relationship.order}' is a duplicate of an existing relationship in the network.
Duplicate Roots
The root '${network.root}' in network '${network.name}' is a duplicate of an existing root already declared in the network.

This error occurs when a network object's roots array contains the same QName more than once. Each value in the roots array must be unique. The constraint applies both to roots declared directly in a named network object and to roots appended via extendTargetName — if an extending network attempts to add a root QName that is already present in the target network's roots array, this error is raised on each such duplicate entry.
Duplicate Label Object
A label for '${label.relatedName}' with language '${label.language}' and label type '${label.labelType}' is already defined.
Unresolved Related Name
The related name '{relatedName}' referenced in label cannot be resolved to a XBRL Model object.
Invalid Language
The language code '{languageCode}' is not a valid BCP47 language code.
Invalid Allowed Object
The object type '{objectType}' is not valid in the allowedObjects property of '{propertyTypeName}'.
Reference Language Redefined
The reference language for '{referenceName}' has been redefined in an extension.
Reference Type Redefined
The reference type for '{referenceName}' has been redefined in an extension.
Invalid Domain Class
The domain class '{domainClass}' cannot be used in this context for domain '{domainName}'.
Concept Domain Class On Non Concept Dimension
The concept domain class '{domainClass}' cannot be used as the domain class on the dimension '{dimensionName}'.
Unit Domain Class On Non Unit Dimension
The unit domain class '{domainClass}' cannot be used as the domain class on the dimension '{dimensionName}'.
Language Domain Class On Non Language Dimension
The language domain class '{domainClass}' cannot be used as the domain class on the dimension '{dimensionName}'.
Domain Class used as a target
The target of an item in a domain network cannot be the domain class '{domainClass}'.
Invalid Domain Class Reference on Member
Member '{memberName}' is used in domain '{domainName}' whose root is '{domainRoot}', but '{domainRoot}' is not listed in the member's domainClasses property.

This error occurs when a member object declares a domainClasses property, and that member is used as a target in a domain whose root (domain class) is not listed in the member's domainClasses array. The domainClasses property constrains which domain class hierarchies a member may participate in. If a member is placed in a domain whose root domain class is absent from its domainClasses list, it indicates a structural inconsistency — the member is being used in a context its domain class declarations do not permit.
Invalid Domain Target
The target '{targetName}' is not valid for domain '{domainName}' with root '{domainClass}'.
Root of domain is not a domain class object.
The domain class '{domainClass}' referenced in domain '{domainName}' is not a domainClass object.
Domain Class Type Mismatches Hierarchical Domain.
The type of the domain class '{domainClass}' does not match the expected type for the domain '{domainName}'.
Invalid Data Type Object
The data type object '{dataTypeName}' is not properly defined.
Invalid Empty Set
The set property '{propertyName}' cannot be empty for object '{objectName}'.
Unit in Non-Unit Domain
The unit '{unitName}' cannot be used in domain '{domainName}' which is not a unit domain.
Inconsistent Taxonomy Defined Dimension Property
The dimension '{dimensionName}' has inconsistent property '{propertyName}' across its definitions.
Dimensions Inconsistent with Base Cube
The dimensions in cube '{cubeName}' are inconsistent with its base cube '{baseCubeName}'.
Unknown Included Object
The included object '{objectName}' cannot be found in taxonomy '{taxonomyName}'.
Invalid Included Object Type
The object type '{objectType}' is not valid for inclusion in importObjectTypes.
Invalid Period Type
The period type '{periodType}' for concept '{conceptName}' is not valid.
Invalid Datatype for Enumeration
The datatype '{dataType}' is not valid for concept '{conceptName}' with enumeration domain. Must be xs:QName.
Fact Value Data Type Mismatch
The fact '{factName}' has value '{value}' which does not match the data type '{dataType}' of concept '{conceptName}'.

This error occurs when a fact value does not conform to the data type of its associated concept. For example, a date value used with a monetary concept, or a text value used with a numeric concept. The fact value must be valid according to the concept's data type definition.
Source ID Not Found
The element with ID '{elementID}' was not found in document '{document}'.

This error occurs when a fact value source specifies references an element ID that does not exist in the source document. The id attribute must specify a valid element ID in the referenced HTML or XML document. If the specified ID is not found, this error is generated.
Ambiguous File Reference

The factValueSource or factValueAnchor object does not specify a 'source' attribute, and the sourceMapping object contains multiple files. When multiple source files are defined, the 'source' attribute must be provided to identify which file to use.

This error occurs when a factValueSource or factValueAnchor object specifies an 'id' property to reference content in a source document, but does not include a 'source' attribute to identify which document file to use. If the sourceMapping object in documentInfo contains only one file, that file is used by default. However, if sourceMapping contains multiple files (e.g., multiple HTML or PDF files), the 'source' attribute becomes mandatory to disambiguate which file contains the referenced ID. Without this disambiguation, the system cannot determine the correct source document, and this error is raised.
Unknown Fact Transformation
The transformation '{transformationName}' specified for fact '{factName}' is not defined in the transform types model.

This error occurs when a fact value source specifies a transformation attribute that is not recognized or defined in the transform types model. All transformations used in value sources must be defined as members in the transform types model. If a transformation name is misspelled or not supported, this error is generated.
Invalid Dimension Member
The object '{memberName}' is not a valid dimension member for dimension '{dimensionName}'.
Invalid Domain Relationship Target
The relationship target '{targetName}' in domain '{domainName}' must be a concept, member, entity, abstract, or unit object.
Invalid Fact Member
The object '{memberName}' cannot be used as a dimension member in a fact.
Invalid Domain Source
The source '{sourceName}' is not valid for domain '{domainName}' with root '{domainClass}'.
Invalid JSON Structure - Missing Required Property
The JSON object {objectNames missing the required property '{propertyName}'.
Invalid JSON Structure - Invalid Property Defined
The property '{propertyName}' is not valid for {objectType} object '{objectName}'.
Disallowed Object Property
The property '{propertyName}' is not allowed for object '{objectName}' based on the allowedObjects constraint.
Disallowed Object Model Type

Object type '{objectType}' is not allowed in modelType '{modelType}'. The object '{firstObjectName}' violates the allowedObjects constraint. This constraint only applies to objects in the document namespace when modelForm is 'module' or undefined.

This error occurs when an xbrlModel with a modelType attribute contains object types that are not in the modelType's allowedObjects array. The constraint only applies to objects defined in the same namespace as the xbrlModel's documentNamespace when modelForm is 'module' or undefined. Objects included via importTaxonomy are not subject to this constraint.
Missing Required Model Type Property
The xbrlModel is missing required property '{requiredProperty}' for modelType '{modelType}'. All properties defined in the modelType's requiredProperties array must be present on the xbrlModel.

This error occurs when an xbrlModel with a modelType attribute does not define all properties specified in the modelType's requiredProperties array. Each property listed in requiredProperties must be included as a property of the xbrlModel for the model to be valid according to the modelType specification.
Cannot Extend Complete Domain
The domain {domainName} is marked as complete and cannot be extended.
Document Namespace Has No Prefix
The documentNamespace '{documentNamespace}' does not match any defined namespace prefix in the namespaces declaration.
Document Namespace Not Defined

This error occurs when an XBRL Model with a modelForm of 'module' (or where modelForm is not defined, which defaults to module) does not define the documentNamespace property in its documentInfo object. The documentNamespace property is required for modules and must be a prefix string defined as a key in the namespaces map.
The documentNamespace property is required for modules but is not defined in the documentInfo object.
Object Namespace Prefix Not Defined
The object namespace prefix '{prefix}' is not defined for object '{objectName}' in the namespace declaration.
Object Namespace Mismatch with Document Namespace
The object namespace prefix '{prefix}' does not match the document namespace for object '{objectName}'.
Invalid Final Taxonomy Modification
The final XBRL Model '{taxonomyName}' cannot be modified by extension XBRL Model '{extensionTaxonomyName}'.
Import Taxonomy Imports Import Taxonomy Object
The objectType property in the selection object includes the xbrl:importTaxonomyObject object type
Model Not Found
The model '{modelName}' could not be found or loaded.
Extension Taxonomy Adds Final Object Type
The extension taxonomy '{extensionTaxonomyName}' attempts to add an object which cannot be extended of '{objectType}'.
Extension Taxonomy Extends Final Object
The extension taxonomy '{extensionTaxonomyName}' attempts to extend an object which cannot be extended of '{objectType}' because it is marked as final.
Invalid Data Type for Domain Class
The datatype '{dataType}' is not valid for domain class '{domainClass}' of domain '{domainName}'.
Invalid Domain Object
The relationship object '{objectName}' is not a valid domain object for domain '{domainName}'.
Multiple Domain Classes
Multiple domain classes are defined for domain '${domain.name}', which is not allowed.
Unallowed Data Type Object
The datatype '{dataType}' is not valid for the object type '{objectType}'.
Collection Type Without Collection Base Type
The collection type '{collectionType}' is specified without a corresponding collectionBaseType.
Invalid Core Dimension

The coreDimensions array in cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' contains '{dimensionName}' which is not a valid core dimension. Only xbrl:concept, xbrl:period, xbrl:entity, xbrl:unit, and xbrl:language are allowed.

This error occurs when a cubeType's coreDimensions array contains a dimension that is not one of the five valid core dimensions (xbrl:concept, xbrl:period, xbrl:entity, xbrl:unit, xbrl:language). Core dimensions must be from the predefined XBRL core dimension set.
Duplicate Core Dimension
The coreDimensions array in cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' contains duplicate dimension QName '{dimensionName}'.

This error occurs when a cubeType's coreDimensions array contains the same dimension QName more than once. Each core dimension must appear at most once in the array to ensure unambiguous cube structure.
Invalid Dimension DataType Property
The dimension constraint object in cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' includes a dataType property but the type property is not set to 'typed'.

This error occurs when a dimension constraint specifies a dataType property but the type property is not set to 'typed'. The dataType property is only valid for typed dimensions and should not be used with explicit dimensions.
Duplicate Dimension in Constraint
The dimension '{dimensionName}' appears multiple times in the cubeDimensionConstraints.allowed array of cubeType '{cubeTypeName}'.

This error occurs when the same dimension appears multiple times in the cubeDimensionConstraints.allowed array. Each dimension can only have one constraint definition within a cubeType to avoid conflicting constraint specifications.
Invalid Relationship Type Reference
The cubeRelationship constraint object in cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' references type '{relationshipTypeName}' which does not resolve to a valid relationshipType object.

This error occurs when a cubeRelationship constraint references a relationship type QName that does not resolve to a valid relationshipType object in the model. All relationship type references must point to existing relationship type definitions.
Invalid Source QName
In cubeType '{cubeTypeName}', cubeRelationship constraint source.qname '{qname}' does not resolve to a valid QName.
This error occurs when a cubeRelationship constraint's source.qname property does not resolve to a valid QName in the model. The source QName must reference an existing model component object.
Invalid Source Object Type
In cubeType '{cubeTypeName}', cubeRelationship constraint source.objectType '{objectType}' does not resolve to a valid model component object.

This error occurs when a cubeRelationship constraint's source.objectType property does not resolve to a valid model component object type. The objectType must reference a recognized model component type in the model.
Invalid Target QName
In cubeType '{cubeTypeName}', cubeRelationship constraint target.qname '{qname}' does not resolve to a valid QName.
This error occurs when a cubeRelationship constraint's target.qname property does not resolve to a valid QName in the model. The target QName must reference an existing model component object.
Invalid Target Object Type
In cubeType '{cubeTypeName}', cubeRelationship constraint target.objectType '{objectType}' does not resolve to a valid model component object.

This error occurs when a cubeRelationship constraint's target.objectType property does not resolve to a valid model component object type. The objectType must reference a recognized model component type in the model.
Invalid Property Type Reference
The property type QName '{propertyTypeName}' in cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' does not resolve to a valid propertyType object.

This error occurs when a cubeProperties constraint references a property type QName that does not resolve to a valid propertyType object in the model. All property type references must point to existing property type definitions.
Core Dimensions Expansion
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' coreDimensions includes '{dimensionName}' which is not in the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}' coreDimensions.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType adds a core dimension to the coreDimensions array that was not present in the base cube type. Derived types cannot expand the set of core dimensions; they can only restrict or maintain them.
Invalid Dimension Closed Expansion
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' sets cubeDimensionConstraints.closed to false, but the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}' has it set to true.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType sets the cubeDimensionConstraints.closed property to false when the base cube type has it set to true. This would expand the allowed dimensions, violating the restriction-only inheritance principle.
Invalid Dimension Requirement Relaxation
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' sets required to false for dimension '{dimensionName}', but the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}' has it set to true.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType changes a dimension's required property from true to false, making it optional. This relaxes the constraint from the base type, violating the restriction-only principle. Derived types cannot make required dimensions optional.
Invalid DataType Expansion

The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' specifies dataType '{dataType}' for dimension '{dimensionName}' which is not the same or more restrictive than the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}' dataType '{baseDataType}'.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType changes the dataType constraint for a dimension to a type that is not the same or more restrictive than the base type. Data type changes must narrow the allowed value space, not expand it.
Invalid Dimension Addition
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' adds dimension '{dimensionName}' to cubeDimensionConstraints.allowed, but the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}' has closed set to true.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType adds a new dimension to the cubeDimensionConstraints.allowed array when the base cube type has the closed property set to true. When closed is true, no additional dimensions may be added.
Missing Required Relationship
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' is missing required relationship type '{relationshipTypeName}' that is required by the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}'.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType omits a relationship type that is marked as required in the base cube type's cubeRelationships.required array. All required relationships from the base type must be maintained in derived types.
Missing Required Cube Property
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' is missing required property '{propertyTypeName}' that is required by the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}'.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType omits a property type that is marked as required in the base cube type's cubeProperties.required array. All required properties from the base type must be maintained in derived types.
Invalid Relationship Expansion
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' includes relationship type '{relationshipTypeName}' in cubeRelationships.allowed that is not permitted by the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}'.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType adds a relationship type to cubeRelationships.allowed that was not present in the base cube type's allowed relationships. Derived types cannot expand the set of allowed relationship types.
Invalid Property Expansion
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' includes property type '{propertyTypeName}' in cubeProperties.allowed that is not permitted by the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}'.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType adds a property type to cubeProperties.allowed that was not present in the base cube type's allowed properties. Derived types cannot expand the set of allowed property types.
Invalid Relationship Constraint Relaxation
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' relaxes the source or target constraints for relationship type '{relationshipTypeName}' defined in the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}'.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType relaxes the source or target constraints of a relationship type that was constrained in the base cube type. For example, moving a required relationship to allowed, or broadening source/target specifications. The error does not occur if the  derived cube type has no cubeRelationships objects defined.
Missing Required Dimension Property
The derived cubeType '{cubeTypeName}' dimension '{dimensionName}' is missing required property '{propertyTypeName}' that is required by the base cube type '{baseCubeTypeName}'.

This error occurs when a derived cubeType omits a property from a dimension's dimensionProperties.required array that was required in the base cube type. All dimension properties marked as required in the base type must be maintained in derived types.

This error is generated when an attempt is made to extend a domain that is marked as complete. A complete domain cannot be extended with additional relationships or members, so any such attempt results in this error.

This error is generated when a cube uses a domainName property or domainDataType on the period dimension. The period dimension is a built in typed dimension and does not support domain assignments, so including a domain property results in an error.

This error is generated when a cube dimension that is based on a concept dimension incorrectly includes the 'allowDomainFacts' property with a value of true. Concept dimensions cannot have domain facts, so this property is not applicable and results in an error.

This error is generated when an object's namespace prefix does not match the document's  namespace for that XBRL Model module. Each defined object must use the namespace prefix defined in the document namespace for a XBRL Model module.

This error occurs when the same object name (QName) is defined more than once in a XBRL Model. Each XBRL Model object must have a unique name within its namespace. Duplicate definitions can arise from defining the same object multiple times in a single XBRL Model file or across imported taxonomies.

This error occurs when the same property is included on an object with conflicting values. A property must only have consistent values when assigned to an object. The same property can be assigned to an object ONLY if the values are the same.

This error is generated when a QName reference in the XBRL Model cannot be resolved to any defined XBRL Model object. This occurs when properties, relationships, or other XBRL Model elements reference an object name that has not been defined in the XBRL Model or its imports.

This error is generated when a QName reference in the XBRL Model resolves to a defined object, but that object is not of the expected type. For example, a cubeType property referencing a concept object instead of a cubeType object, or an extendTargetName referencing a domain instead of the expected object type.

This error occurs when a property value does not conform to its expected datatype or constraints. For example, a boolean property receiving a non-boolean value, a numeric property receiving non-numeric text, or an enumerated property receiving a value outside the allowed set.

This error is generated when a fact's dimensions and properties do not match any cube's fact space defined in the XBRL Model. Every fact must fit within at least one cube's dimensional constraints. This ensures that facts can be properly validated and interpreted within the dimensional model.

This error occurs when a cube has its cubeComplete property set to true but one or more fact spaces defined by the cube contain no facts. When a cube is marked as complete, all its defined fact spaces must be populated with at least one fact to ensure the reported data is comprehensive and complete.
This error occurs when a dimension object in the XBRL Model is not properly defined according to the specification. A valid dimension object must have a domainClass property.
This error code has been replaced by oimte:invalidQNameType, which covers all cases where a QName reference resolves to an object of an incorrect type.

This error is generated when a cube specifies a domain name for a dimension that does not match the domainClass on the dimension and the root attribute of the domain. The domain name must reference a defined domainClass object in the XBRL Model that is appropriate for the dimension type.
This error occurs when the value of the excludeCube property in a cube is the same as the cube itself. A cube cannot exclude itself.

This error occurs when a cube includes the same cube in both the excludeCubes and requiredCubes properties. A cube cannot simultaneously exclude and require the same cube, as these properties have contradictory semantics.

This error occurs when a cube's excludeCubes and requiredCubes properties reference cubes that share the same dimensional space. While the cubes may be different, if they define overlapping dimensional spaces, this creates a logical conflict where facts would be both required and excluded simultaneously.

This error occurs when a cube specifies required cubes in the requiredCubes property but one or more dimensional spaces defined by a required cube contain no matching dimensional space in the base cube. When a cube is included in requiredCubes, all its defined dimensional spaces must be present in the base cube.

This error occurs when a cube is missing a dimension that is required. In the XBRL Model, certain cube types or configurations may require specific dimensions to be present. For example, a cube extending a base cube must include all required dimensions from the base cube.

This error occurs when a cube is missing the concept dimension. In the XBRL model all cubes must include a concept dimension so that the concepts associated with the cube can be defined. A default cube must still define the dimension but does not need to include a domain name for the concept dimension.

This error is generated when a cube includes a dimension that is not permitted for that cube's type or configuration. Different cube types may have restrictions on which dimensions can be included based on the cube's purpose and the XBRL Model's design rules.
This error is generated when a cube includes a dimension that is duplicated. Each dimension must be unique within a cube.

This error occurs when an object is declared with an invalid or unrecognized object type. The XBRL Model specification defines specific object types (concept, member, dimension, cube, etc.), and all XBRL Model objects must use one of these defined types.

This error is generated when a non period dimension has period constraints. Period constraints define what values are allowed for a dimension, and these constraints must only be defined for period dimensions.

This error is generated when the timeSpan property is used together with both the endDate and startDate properties in a period constraint. The timeSpan property defines a duration and should not be redundantly specified when both endpoints are already defined.

This error is generated when a domain declares a root but that root object is never used as a source in any of the domain's relationships. Every domain class must appear as the source of at least one relationship, otherwise it serves no purpose in defining the domain's member hierarchy.

This error occurs when a network declares root nodes that do not match the actual root nodes in the network's relationship structure. A network root must be a node that has no incoming relationships within that network, forming the top-level entry points of the network's hierarchy.

This error occurs when a network contains two or more relationship objects whose `source`, `target`, `order`, and any additional properties are all identical. A duplicate can arise directly within a single network definition or when an extension taxonomy uses `extendTargetName` to append a relationship that already exists in the base network.

This error is generated when multiple labels with the same relatedName, language, and labelType are defined in the XBRL Model. Each label must be uniquely identified by the combination of these three properties. Having duplicate labels creates ambiguity in which label should be used.

This error occurs when a label's relatedName property references a XBRL Model object that does not exist. Every label must be associated with a valid XBRL Model object (concept, dimension, member, etc.). The relatedName must resolve to an object defined in the XBRL Model or its imports.

This error is generated when a label uses a language code that is not a valid BCP 47 language identifier. Language codes must conform to the BCP 47 standard to ensure proper internationalization and interoperability. Examples of valid codes include 'en', 'fr', 'de', 'ja'.

This error occurs when a property type's allowedObjects list contains an invalid or unrecognized object type. The allowedObjects property constrains which types of XBRL Model objects can use a property. Valid object types are any referenceable model object. These are objects that have  QName such as xbrl:memberObject, xbrl:conceptObject, xbrl:unitObject, xbrl:entityObject, xbrl:groupObject etc.

This error occurs when an extension XBRL Model attempts to redefine the language property of a reference object. Like other core reference properties, the language cannot be changed in extension taxonomies. References must maintain their language specification from their original definition.

This error is generated when an extension XBRL Model attempts to redefine the type property of a reference object. Reference types are fixed at definition and cannot be modified by extension taxonomies. This ensures consistency in how references are interpreted across the XBRL Model.
This error occurs when a concept domain class is used on a dimension that is not the concept dimension. The concept domain class xbrl:conceptDomain can only be used with the dimension xbrl:concept.
This error occurs when a unit domain class is used on a dimension that is not the unit dimension. The unit domain class xbrl:unitDomain can only be used with the dimension xbrl:unit.

This error occurs when a language domain class is used on a dimension that is not the language dimension. The language domain class xbrl:languageDomain can only be used with the dimension xbrl:language.
This error occurs when a domain or a dimension references a root object that is not a domain class object. The domain class must be a defined domain class object in the XBRL Model.
This error occurs when the domain class is used as a target in any relationships within the domain. The domain class can only be a source.

This error is generated when a relationship within a domain references a target object that does not exist or is not of an appropriate type. Domain relationship targets must be concepts, members, entities, abstracts, or units depending on the domain's configuration.

This error occurs when a domain references a root object by QName that is not defined as a domainClassObject. The domain class attribute defined as part of the domainObject must be defined as a domainClass object in the XBRL Model or imported XBRL Models.
This error occurs when a domain class defines a type for the domain using the attribute allowedDomainItems and the domain associated with the DomainClass includes a hierarchy.

This error is generated when a datatype object is not properly defined according to the specification. Datatype objects must have valid base types, appropriate restrictions or extensions, and consistent property definitions. The datatype definition must conform to XML Schema datatype semantics.

This error occurs when a collection property that requires at least one element is defined as empty. Certain XBRL Model structures require non-empty collections to be valid. For example, some configurations may require at least one relationship, dimension, or other element to be present.

This error is generated when a unit object is used in a domain that is not designated as a unit domain. Units can only be included in domains that have a unit domain class. Using units in other domain types violates the XBRL Model's structural integrity.

This error is generated when a dimension is defined with inconsistent properties across different parts of the XBRL Model. When a dimension appears in multiple cubes or contexts, its fundamental properties (such as type, domain, etc.) must remain consistent throughout the XBRL Model.

This error occurs when a cube that extends or references a base cube has dimensions that are inconsistent with the base cube's dimensional structure. Extension cubes must maintain compatibility with their base cubes' dimensional definitions to ensure proper fact space inheritance.

This error occurs when an import profile's includedObjects list references an object that does not exist in the imported taxonomy. All objects listed for inclusion must be defined in the source taxonomy. This ensures that selective imports reference valid, available objects.

This error is generated when an import profile's importObjectTypes property specifies an object type that is not valid for selective import. Only certain object types can be selectively imported. The specification defines which object types support this filtering capability.

This error is generated when a concept has a periodType property value that is not valid. The periodType must be either 'instant' or 'duration'. This property determines the temporal characteristics of facts using this concept and is essential for proper fact validation.

This error occurs when a property type has an enumeration domain specified but does not use xs:QName as its dataType. Property types with enumeration domains must have xs:QName dataType because enumeration members are referenced by their QNames within the model.

This error is generated when an object used as a dimension member is not valid for that dimension. Explicit dimensions require members from their associated domain, while typed dimensions require values that conform to their domain datatype. The member must be appropriate for the dimension's type.

This error is generated when a relationship in a domain specifies a target that is not an allowed object type. Domain relationships must target concepts, members, entities, abstracts, or units. Using other object types as relationship targets violates the domain's structural constraints.

This error occurs when a fact uses a dimension member that is not valid according to the model constraints. The member must be part of the dimension's domain (for explicit dimensions) or must conform to the dimension's datatype (for typed dimensions). The fact's dimensional coordinates must be valid.

This error is generated when a relationship within a domain uses a source object that is not valid. The source in domain relationships must be a member of the domain and must be of an appropriate object type. Domain classes and extension targets must be properly connected through valid source objects.

This error occurs when a required property defined by the XBRL model specification is missing from a model object. All required properties must be present for each object type to ensure the model is valid and complete according to the specification.

This error occurs when an object in the model defines a property that is not recognized or allowed by the schema. Only properties defined in the specification should be present on each object type. Extra or misspelled properties indicate a structural issue with the model.

This error occurs when a property is assigned to an object that is not in the property type's allowedObjects list. Each property type defines which object types can use it. Attempting to use a property on an incompatible object type should raise this error.
The intervalOfMeasurement property value MUST be parsable as a duration interval according to `xs:duration`. If not, processors MUST raise an error.
The specified model  could not be found. This error occurs when a reference is made to a model that does not exist or is not accessible.
The oimte:intervalOfMeasurement property on a non-date/time domain MUST be treated as an error.
Invalid Taxonomy Group Source
The source '{sourceName}' in the group tree relationship is not valid. The source must be either a taxonomy object or a group object.

This error is generated when a relationship in the group tree object has a source that is neither a taxonomy object nor a group object. Group tree relationships can originate from the taxonomy itself (for top-level groups) or from group objects (for nested groups within the hierarchy).
Invalid Taxonomy Group Target
The target '{targetName}' in the group tree relationship is not valid. The target must be a group object.

This error occurs when a relationship in the group tree object has a target that is not a group object. All group tree relationships must target group objects, as the purpose of the group tree is to organize groups hierarchically. Only group objects are valid targets for these relationships.
Group Tree Not Importable
The group tree object cannot be imported from taxonomy '{taxonomyName}'. When a taxonomy is imported, the group tree object is not imported with it.

This error is generated when an attempt is made to import a group tree object from another taxonomy. The group tree is specific to each taxonomy and defines how that taxonomy's groups are organized. When importing a taxonomy, the group tree object is intentionally excluded. Each taxonomy must define its own group tree if needed.

This error occurs when the documentNamespace property specifies a prefix that is not defined in the namespaces declaration. The documentNamespace must reference a valid prefix that exists in the namespaces map.
This error occurs when an object's QName uses a namespace prefix that is not defined in the taxonomy's defined documentNamespace declaration.

This error is generated when an extension taxonomy attempts to modify an object from a final taxonomy. Objects defined in a final taxonomy are immutable and cannot be changed by extension taxonomies. Any attempt to alter properties, relationships, or definitions of final taxonomy objects must raise this error. Any attempt to add new objects types must also raise this error.
The objectType property in the selection object must not include the xbrl:importTaxonomyObject object type. If it does, processors must raise an error.
Conflicting Import Properties
The import taxonomy object has excludeLabels set to true and the selections array includes a selection object with objectType xbrl:labelObject.

This error occurs when an import taxonomy object in the importedTaxonomies array has the excludeLabels property set to true and the selections array includes a selection object whose objectType is xbrl:labelObject. These two properties are contradictory: excludeLabels suppresses all label imports while the selection explicitly requests them. Processors must raise this error when both conditions are present on the same import taxonomy object.
Invalid Selection Expression
A selections entry is structurally invalid, references an unrecognized objectType, contains an unknown operator, or uses an operator incompatible with the property's value type.

This error is raised when a structured select statement in the selections array of an importTaxonomy object or finalTaxonomy object is invalid. The following conditions each independently produce this error: (1) the objectType property is absent or is not a syntactically valid QName; (2) the objectType QName does not identify a recognized model component object type; (3) a where filter entry is missing the required property, operator, or value fields; (4) the operator value is not one of the ten enumerated operators (==, !=, in, not in, contains, not contains, >, <, >=, <=); (5) the operator is incompatible with the effective type of the referenced property (e.g. contains applied to a boolean property); or (6) the value for an in or not in operator is not a JSON array. See the Selection Expression Grammar appendix for the normative formal specification.

This error is generated when an extension taxonomy adds an object that has been defined as final in an imported taxonomy. Objects defined as final cannot be redefined or extended in any way by extension taxonomies. Any attempt to add a new object of a type that is final must raise this error.

This error is generated when an extension taxonomy extends an object using the extend property that has been defined as final in an imported taxonomy. Objects defined as final cannot be redefined or extended in any way by extension taxonomies. Any attempt to extend an object that is final must raise this error.

This error occurs when a domainDataType property used on a cubeDimension object has a data type that is not defined in the allowedDomainItems array of the domain class object. The data types specified for the domain class must be compatible with the domainDataType assigned to the cube dimension.

This error occurs when a domain object is used when its type is prohibited by the values defined in the allowedDomainItems property of the domain class object. This covers: (1) any object used as a source or target in a domain relationship that is not permitted by the domain class's allowedDomainItems; (2) objects used as sources in a concept domain that are not a concept object, abstract object, or xbrl:conceptDomain; (3) objects used in an entity domain relationship that are not entity objects or xbrl:entityDomain; and (4) objects used in a unit domain relationship that are not unit objects or xbrl:unitDomain.
This error occurs when a domain object has multiple domain class objects defined. Each domain must have exactly one domain class object.
This error occurs when a model object has a data type that is not allowed based on the definition of  the allowedObjects property of the data type.
This error occurs when a collection type is defined with a baseType property that is not xbrli:collection.
Test File Name
Status

Import Taxonomy Errors
Group Tree Errors
JSON Errors
Canonical Form Requires Compiled Model
Only compiled models can have canonical forms. The model '{modelName}' has modelForm '{modelForm}' which cannot be converted to canonical form.

Raised when an attempt is made to convert a module-form model to canonical form. Canonical form is only supported for compiled models. Check that the modelForm property is set to "compiled" before applying canonical form processing.
Non Semantic Property In Canonical Form
A canonical model MUST NOT contain non-semantic properties. Property '{propertyName}' found in model '{modelName}'.

Raised when a canonical model contains properties that are classified as non-semantic (such as presentation-only or operational metadata). A canonical model must contain only semantic properties that define the meaning and structure of the model.
Unsorted Array In Canonical Form
All arrays in a canonical model must be sorted. Array in '{objectName}' is not sorted according to the canonical array ordering rules.

Raised when arrays in a canonical model are not sorted according to the canonical array ordering rules. All arrays in canonical models must be deterministically ordered to enable reliable comparison between models.
Invalid JSON Structure
The JSON structure of the XBRL model is invalid: '{reason}'.
Raised when the overall JSON structure of an XBRL model document is invalid in a way that does not fit a more specific error category. This is a general structural validation error.
Imported Taxonomy Defined For Compiled Taxonomy
The model '{modelName}' has modelForm 'compiled' but contains an importTaxonomy object, which is not allowed.
Raised when an importTaxonomy object is found in a model with modelForm set to "compiled". Compiled models are fully self-contained and cannot import from external taxonomy modules.
Extended Target Name Defined For Model Taxonomy
The domain '{domainName}' has an extendTargetName property defined but the model modelForm is 'module', which does not allow extension.
Raised when a domain object in a module-type taxonomy uses the extendTargetName property. Extension of existing objects is only permitted in importing taxonomies, not in the base module itself.
Invalid Negative Cube Reference
The excludeCubes property of cube '{cubeName}' references '{referenceName}' which is not a cube with a cubeType of xbrl:negativeCube.
Raised when the excludeCubes property of a cube references a cube that does not have a cubeType of xbrl:negativeCube. Only negative cube types can be used to exclude dimensional spaces from a cube.
Invalid Constraint Cube
The constraint cube '{cubeName}' is not valid in the current context.
Raised when a constraint cube reference is invalid or cannot be resolved in the current context.
Exclude Cubes On Negative Cube
The cube '{cubeName}' has cubeType xbrl:negativeCube but also specifies an excludeCubes property, which is not allowed.

Raised when a cube with cubeType xbrl:negativeCube specifies the excludeCubes property. A negative cube is itself a subtraction from another cube; defining excludeCubes on a negative cube would create double-negation semantics and is prohibited.
Instant With Duration Properties
The period dimension in cube '{cubeName}' has periodType 'instant' but also has timeSpan or startDate properties defined, which are only valid for duration periods.
Raised when a period dimension constraint has a periodType of "instant" but also defines timeSpan or startDate properties. These properties are only applicable to duration periods.
Redundant Period Pattern Property
The period dimension in cube '{cubeName}' has a periodPattern property that conflicts with other period constraint properties (timeSpan, endDate, or startDate).

Raised when a period pattern property conflicts with other period constraint properties such as timeSpan, endDate, or startDate. When periodPattern represents a duration, it cannot be combined with these properties.
Redundant Time Shift Property
The timeShift property requires one of conceptName, context, value, or dateProperty to be specified. None were found in cube '{cubeName}'.
Raised when a timeShift property is specified without an accompanying conceptName, context, value, or dateProperty. These properties provide the date reference that timeShift modifies.
Mutually Exclusive Date Resolution Property
Only one of the properties conceptName, context, value, or dateProperty can be present in a period constraint. Multiple were found in cube '{cubeName}'.
Raised when more than one of the mutually exclusive date resolution properties (conceptName, context, value, dateProperty) are specified in the same period constraint. Only one may be present.
Invalid Cube Dimension Type
The dimension '{dimensionName}' in cube '{cubeName}' has an invalid type specification.
Raised when a cube dimension has an invalid type specification that does not conform to the allowed dimension types.
Invalid Concept Data Type
The concept '{conceptName}' referenced as a period context date must have a data type derived from xs:date.

Raised when a concept referenced in a period context specification (conceptName or context property) does not have a data type derived from xs:date. Period date references must use date-typed concepts.
Invalid Concept Domain
The concept dimension in cube '{cubeName}' cannot have a domainDataType property. The concept dimension only supports a domainName.

Raised when the concept dimension in a cube definition includes a domainDataType property, which is not permitted. The concept dimension uses domainName to reference the concept domain, not domainDataType.
Complete Time Series On Non Time Series Dimension
The completeTimeSeries property cannot be used on dimension '{dimensionName}' which is not a time series dimension in cube '{cubeName}'.
Raised when the completeTimeSeries property is applied to a dimension that is not a designated time series dimension. This property is only valid on time series dimensions.
Incomplete Time Series
The time series for concept '{conceptName}' is incomplete. Not all expected intervals have corresponding facts.
Raised when a time series dimension with completeTimeSeries set to true does not have facts for all expected time intervals. Every interval defined by the time series must have a corresponding fact.
Interval Convention On Non Time Series Dimension
The intervalConvention property cannot be set on dimension '{dimensionName}' which is not a time series dimension in cube '{cubeName}'.
Raised when the intervalConvention property is applied to a dimension that is not a designated time series dimension. This property is only valid on time series dimensions.
Time Series Type On Non Time Series Dimension
The timeSeriesType property cannot be used on dimension '{dimensionName}' which is not a time series dimension in cube '{cubeName}'.
Raised when the timeSeriesType property is applied to a dimension that is not a designated time series dimension. This property is only valid on time series dimensions.
Invalid Interval Convention
The intervalConvention value '{value}' is not a valid interval convention for dimension '{dimensionName}'.
Raised when the value of the intervalConvention property is not a valid interval convention value. Valid values are defined by the xbrli:intervalConventionType enumeration.
Missing Interval Excluded
The excludeIntervals property is missing for the time series dimension '{dimensionName}' in cube '{cubeName}'.
Raised when a time series dimension is missing the required excludeIntervals property.
Missing Interval Of Measurement
The intervalOfMeasurement property is required for time series dimension '{dimensionName}' in cube '{cubeName}'.
Raised when the intervalOfMeasurement property is missing from a time series dimension where it is required.
Missing Interval Of Measurement For Complete Series
The intervalOfMeasurement property is required when completeTimeSeries is true for dimension '{dimensionName}' in cube '{cubeName}'.

Raised when the completeTimeSeries property is set to true but the intervalOfMeasurement property is not specified. The interval of measurement is required to determine which intervals must have facts.
Missing Interval Of Measurement For Convention
The intervalOfMeasurement property is required when intervalConvention is specified for dimension '{dimensionName}' in cube '{cubeName}'.
Raised when the intervalConvention property is specified but the intervalOfMeasurement property is not. The interval of measurement is required when specifying a convention.
Missing Aggregation Property Type
The aggregation property is required for the time series dimension '{dimensionName}' in cube '{cubeName}' but was not found.
Raised when a time series dimension requiring an aggregation property type does not have one defined.
Invalid Cube Relationship
The cube type '{cubeTypeName}' has an invalid relationship definition: '{reason}'.
Raised when a cube type defines a relationship constraint that references an invalid or unknown relationship type.
Conflicting Type Definitions
The data type '{dataTypeName}' cannot have both a collectionTypeObject and a mapTypeObject defined simultaneously.
Raised when a data type object has both a collectionTypeObject and a mapTypeObject defined. A data type can only be one kind of structured type - either a collection or a map, not both.
Missing Collection Type Object
The data type '{dataTypeName}' has a baseType of xbrli:collection but is missing the required collectionTypeObject property.

Raised when a data type has a baseType of xbrli:collection but does not include the required collectionTypeObject property. All collection-based data types must define their collection characteristics.
Map Type Without Map Base Type
A mapType object was found on data type '{dataTypeName}' but its baseType is not xbrli:map.
Raised when a mapTypeObject is defined on a data type whose baseType is not xbrli:map. The mapTypeObject can only appear on data types with xbrli:map as their base type.
Missing Map Type Object
The data type '{dataTypeName}' has a baseType of xbrli:map but is missing the required mapTypeObject property.
Raised when a data type has a baseType of xbrli:map but does not include the required mapTypeObject property. All map-based data types must define their key and value type characteristics.
Disallowed Map Key
The key '{key}' in map value for property '{propertyName}' is not in the allowedKeys set defined for data type '{dataTypeName}'.
Raised when a map value contains a key that is not in the allowedKeys set defined for the map data type. If allowedKeys is specified, only listed keys are permitted.
Duplicate Map Key
The key '{key}' appears multiple times in map value for property '{propertyName}'. Map keys must be unique.
Raised when a map value contains duplicate keys. By default (or when uniqueKeys is true), all keys in a map must be unique.
Required Key Not In Allowed Keys
The required key '{key}' in data type '{dataTypeName}' is not present in the allowedKeys set.
Raised when a key listed in the requiredKeys set of a map data type is not present in the allowedKeys set. All required keys must also be allowed keys.
Invalid Map Key Type
The key '{key}' in map value for property '{propertyName}' is not a valid value of the keyType '{keyType}'.
Raised when a key in a map value is not a valid value of the keyType data type defined for the map. All keys must conform to the specified key type.
Invalid Map Value Type
The value for key '{key}' in map value for property '{propertyName}' is not a valid value of the valueType '{valueType}'.
Raised when a value in a map is not a valid value of the valueType data type defined for the map. All values must conform to the specified value type.
Invalid Data Type
The data type '{dataTypeName}' is invalid or cannot be applied in this context.
Raised when a data type reference is invalid, cannot be resolved, or is used in a context where it is not applicable.
Inconsistent Properties
The concept '{conceptName}' has properties that are inconsistent with those of its superclass '{superclassName}'.
Raised when a concept that is defined as a subclass has properties that conflict with those of its superclass. Subclass concept properties must be a subset of the superclass properties.
Concept Properties Inconsistent With Relationship
The concept '{conceptName}' has properties that are inconsistent with the constraints of the relationship type '{relationshipTypeName}'.

Raised when a concept has properties that are inconsistent with the constraints defined by the relationship type that uses the concept. For example, an instant-inflow relationship must have a source with a instant periodType and a target with a duration periodType.
Concept Properties Inconsistent With Summation Item Relationship
The concept '{conceptName}' has properties that are inconsistent with the constraints of the summation-item relationship type '{relationshipTypeName}'.

Raised when a concept has properties that are inconsistent with the constraints defined by the summation-item relationship type that uses the concept. For example, a calculation relationship source concept must have a periodType that matches the periodType of the target concept, a weight of 1 must use consistent xbrla:balance properties and a weight of -1 must use different xbrla:balance properties.
Invalid Unit Data Type
The unit '{unitName}' has a data type '{dataType}' that does not match the data type of the concept '{conceptName}' it is associated with.
Raised when a unit object has a data type that does not match the expected data type for the concept it is associated with. Units must be compatible with the data type of the concept.
Invalid Unit Object
The unit '{unitName}' referenced in compositeUnitRepresentation is not a valid unit object.
Raised when a reference to a unit object in the compositeUnitRepresentation property cannot be resolved to a valid unit object in the taxonomy model.
Unit Self-Reference in Definition
Unit '{unitName}' references itself in compositeUnitRepresentation '{value}'. A unit MUST NOT appear in its own unit string representation.

Raised when a unit object's compositeUnitRepresentation contains a unit string representation that includes the unit's own QName (its name property). A unit MUST NOT reference itself — directly or as a component of a composite expression — in its own compositeUnitRepresentation, as this would create a circular unit definition.
Missing SQName Property
Entity object '{entityName}' is missing the required SQName property.
Raised when an entity object does not have a required SQName property. All entity objects must have a unique qualified name identifier.
Missing Required Property
The reference object '{referenceName}' is missing the required property '{propertyName}' as defined by the referenceType.
Raised when a reference object does not include a property that is defined as required by the referenceType. All required properties defined in the referenceType must be present in the reference.
Invalid Required Property
The referenceType '{referenceTypeName}' includes required property '{propertyName}' which cannot be used on a reference object.
Raised when a referenceType specifies a required property that is not valid for use on reference objects. Required properties must be applicable to the reference object type.
Invalid Ordered Property
The referenceType '{referenceTypeName}' includes ordered property '{propertyName}' which cannot be used on a reference object.
Raised when a referenceType specifies an ordered property that is not valid for use on reference objects. Ordered properties must be applicable to the reference object type.
Taxonomy Group In Network
The taxonomy-group relationship type is only valid in a groupTree object and cannot be used in network '{networkName}'.

Raised when a taxonomy-group relationship type is used in a network object. The xbrl:taxonomy-group relationship type is reserved for use within groupTree objects and must not appear in regular networks.
Invalid Calculation Relationship
The calculation relationship in network '{networkName}' references concept '{conceptName}' which is not in the domain of the associated cube.
Raised when a calculation relationship in a network references a concept that is not in the domain of the cube associated with the network. Calculation relationships are only validated against cubes.
Invalid Group Object
The groupName '{groupName}' in the groupContent object does not reference a valid group object in the XBRL model.
Raised when a groupContent object references a group QName that does not correspond to a valid group object in the XBRL model.
Hash Without Metadata
The factSource '{factSourceName}' has a hash property but no metadata property. The metadata property is recommended to enable interoperability.

Raised when a factSource object has a hash property but no metadata property. While not strictly required, the metadata property is strongly recommended when using hash-based fact storage to enable interoperability between processors.
Metadata Without Hash
The factSource '{factSourceName}' has a metadata property but no hash property. The hash property is required when metadata is present.

Raised when a factSource object has a metadata property but no hash property. The hash property is required whenever metadata is present, as metadata is only meaningful in the context of hash-based fact storage.
Empty Concept Set
The factSource metadata for '{factSourceName}' has an empty concepts set. At least one concept must be specified.
Raised when the factSource metadata has an empty concepts set. The concepts array must contain at least one concept QName that is represented in the fact collection.
Invalid Fact Count
The factSource metadata for '{factSourceName}' has an invalid factCount value. The factCount must be greater than zero.
Raised when the factCount value in factSource metadata is not greater than zero. A factSource must contain at least one fact.
Missing Concept Dimension
The factSource metadata for '{factSourceName}' does not include the required xbrl:concept dimension in the dimensions set.
Raised when the factSource metadata dimensions set does not include the required xbrl:concept dimension. Every fact collection must have facts with a concept dimension.
Invalid Member Count
The factSource dimension summary for '{dimensionName}' in '{factSourceName}' has an invalid memberCount value. The memberCount must be greater than zero.
Raised when the memberCount value in a factSource dimension summary is not greater than zero. Each dimension summary must record at least one member.
Too Many Sample Members
The factSource dimension summary for '{dimensionName}' in '{factSourceName}' has too many sampleMembers. The maximum allowed is 100.
Raised when the sampleMembers array in a factSource dimension summary contains more than 100 items. The sample members array is limited to 100 entries to keep metadata concise.
Member Range On Explicit Dimension
The factSource dimension summary for '{dimensionName}' in '{factSourceName}' has a memberRange property but the dimension is not a typed dimension.

Raised when a factSource dimension summary includes a memberRange property for a dimension that is not a typed dimension. The memberRange property is only applicable to typed dimensions with orderable values.
Unsupported Decimal Suffix Feature
The decimal suffix feature (column decimal suffix) is not supported. It was found in table template '{templateName}'.
Raised when the decimal suffix feature from the XBRL-CSV specification is encountered in a table template. This feature is not supported in the XBRL model specification.
Invalid Column Name
The column name '{columnName}' in table template '{templateName}' is not a valid NCName or contains a prohibited . character.
Raised when a column name in a table template is not a valid NCName or contains the prohibited period (.) character.
Invalid Grid Axis Table Type
The gridHeader object in layout '{layoutName}' can only be used with a gridLayout tableType.
Raised when a gridHeader object is used with a table type that is not gridLayout. Grid headers can only be used with gridLayout table definitions.
Invalid Grid Header Span
The span value in layout '{layoutName}' must be a positive integer.
Raised when the span value of a grid header is not a positive integer. Grid header spans must be positive integers indicating how many columns or rows the header covers.
Invalid Grid Header Span Level
The span property must not be defined where labelLevel has a value of 1 in layout '{layoutName}'.
Raised when a span property is defined on a grid header at labelLevel 1. Grid headers at the first label level cannot have a span because there is nothing for them to span across.
Missing Grid Coordinates
The table point in layout '{layoutName}' must specify at least xAxis and yAxis grid coordinates.
Raised when a table point definition does not specify at least xAxis and yAxis coordinates. Grid table points require a minimum of x and y axis specifications to identify their location.
Invalid Dimension In Table Point
The dimension '{dimensionName}' specified in the table point of layout '{layoutName}' does not correspond to a valid dimension in the taxonomy.
Raised when a dimension referenced in a table point definition does not correspond to any dimension defined in the taxonomy. All dimension references in layout objects must be resolvable.
